title: cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera
intro: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera, commonly known as Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Red-osier Dogwood are attributed to its therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera have been shown to exhibit anti-diabetic, anti-cancer, and anti-aging effects, making it a valuable plant for further research and potential medicinal applications.
benefits: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has many benefits, such as its ability to provide medicinal properties that can help lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. The plant's extracts have also been shown to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds and preventing infections. Furthermore, Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, demonstrating its potential as a natural remedy. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced well-being, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, wound healing, and cancer prevention. The plant's extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation, improve wound closure rates, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells, ultimately improving the quality of life for those affected. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and even reduce the risk of age-related diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera, people can enjoy improved overall health, enhanced physical performance, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, leading to a more fulfilling and active daily life.
constituents: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, as well as triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The phenolic acids present in this plant, including caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid, have been found to possess antimicrobial and antidiabetic effects, contributing to the management of various diseases. Additionally, the saponins and glycosides present in Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera have been reported to exhibit anti-hypertensive and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hypertension and reduce inflammation, ultimately improving cardiovascular health and overall well-being. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, individuals can potentially benefit from improved digestive health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced quality of life.
parts: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has many medicinal parts, such as the bark, leaves, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The bark of Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera contains flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of this plant contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain and inflammation. The roots of Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera contain iridoid glycosides, including cornuside, which has been used to treat fever and gastrointestinal issues.
preparations: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea is used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, while the tincture is used to treat inflammation and reduce pain. The infusion is also used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Additionally, the plant's berries can be made into a syrup or jam, which is used to treat sore throats and coughs due to its antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. Ingesting its berries or leaves can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of saponins and other toxic compounds. Topical application of its bark or stems can cause skin rashes, itching, and redness, particularly in people with sensitive skin. If ingested in large quantities, Cornus sericea subsp. stolonifera can also cause cardiac problems and interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners.
